title: Berlin Layover - Quick Summary
items: **A Berlin layover is worth leaving the airport for once you have about 5 hours or more.** BER sits close to the historic centre, but the airport itself takes time to leave and re-enter, so plan around the buffers, not the short train ride.

**Check you are allowed out first.** If you are travelling within the Schengen area there is usually no passport control, and most visa-exempt travellers can leave freely. A few nationalities need an airport transit visa, which means staying airside.
**Give yourself real buffers.** Be back at BER security about 90 minutes before a Schengen departure and closer to 2 hours before a non-Schengen one, and count 45 minutes on the train each way.
**The S9 runs straight to Alexanderplatz** from the station under Terminal 1, no change needed. You need an ABC ticket because the airport is in zone C; a single is 5.00 euros in 2026.

**From Alexanderplatz the main sights line up on one flat walk:** the TV Tower, Rotes Rathaus, Berliner Dom, Museum Island, Unter den Linden and the Brandenburg Gate. That core is comfortably a couple of hours on foot.
**Store your bag before you walk.** BER has lockers in Terminal 1, and the big city stations including Alexanderplatz have lockers too, so you can lock a bag and start walking straight from the S9.
